view_matching_logic.Elevation: Check only Elevation views that visibly show a building door, door opening, door frame, door jamb, or full-height door/opening condition associated with the millwork scope. Do not check every Elevation. Cabinet-only elevations, countertop-only elevations, wall-finish-only elevations, media wall elevations, appliance elevations, or elevations with no visible building door/opening condition shall be omitted.

geometry_logic.boundary_detection: This rule applies only to Elevation views. In an Elevation, a building door may appear as a full-height rectangular architectural opening, door frame, jamb, door panel, or diagonal door-leaf graphic. If this door geometry is complete and clear, geometry_applicable must be true. If any possible door-like full-height rectangular opening or diagonal door-leaf graphic is partially visible, faint, cropped, or ambiguous, do not return OMITTED; return REVIEW_REQUIRED. Return OMITTED only when there is no visible or partial evidence of a building door/opening anywhere in the Elevation.
wall_interaction_conditions: The detected view is an Elevation.
A graphical building door/opening condition is visible in the same Elevation.
A valid door condition may appear as a full-height rectangular outline, door frame, jamb, door panel, door leaf, diagonal door-panel graphics, or door-like full-height opening.
Door text, door tag, or door swing arc is not mandatory if graphical door/opening geometry is visible.
A full-height rectangular architectural opening containing diagonal door-leaf graphics shall be treated as a valid building door.
Door text, door tag, or swing arc is optional and not required.

If a tall side rectangular opening with diagonal graphics is visible near the edge of an Elevation, do not classify it as cabinet construction with high confidence. If uncertain whether it is a building door or cabinet/millwork panel, return REVIEW_REQUIRED, not OMITTED.
Cabinet doors, drawer fronts, appliance fronts, and millwork panels shall not be interpreted as building doors.
Cabinet doors, drawer fronts, appliance doors, and hardware schedule references shall not activate this rule.
geometry_logic.expected_boundary_region: The expected checking region is the building door/opening/frame/jamb area within the same Elevation view. The DOOR BY OTHERS note must belong to the same Elevation crop and must not be copied from another view.

pass_condition: The detected view is an Elevation.
The same Elevation shows a graphical building door/opening condition such as full-height rectangular outline, door frame, jamb, door panel, door leaf, or diagonal door-panel graphics.
The required DOOR BY OTHERS note or accepted variant is visibly present in the same Elevation near the door/opening/frame region.
Detected note evidence, detected_text, and coordinates are non-empty and belong to the same view_id/crop.
Confidence score is greater than or equal to 75.
fail_condition: The detected view is an Elevation.
The same Elevation shows a graphical building door/opening condition such as full-height rectangular outline, door frame, jamb, door panel, door leaf, or diagonal door-panel graphics.
The required DOOR BY OTHERS note is missing from that same Elevation.
Failure is not allowed based on Elevation type alone; the building door/opening condition must be active.
The Elevation contains a graphical building door represented by a full-height rectangular opening with architectural door-leaf graphics, but the required DOOR BY OTHERS note is missing.

Failure is allowed when a graphical building door representation (including a full-height rectangular door with diagonal door-leaf symbol) is clearly visible but the required DOOR BY OTHERS note is missing.
review_required_condition: Door scope is applicable but text is unclear, partially readable, or unreadable.
Door object is visible but note association is ambiguous.
Multiple nearby notes create confusion about whether the door is by others.
A possible building door/opening is partially visible, faint, clipped, or ambiguous.
A possible full-height rectangular opening or diagonal door-leaf graphic is visible but cannot be confidently distinguished from millwork or wall panel graphics.
The view contains a door-like vertical rectangular object near the side of the Elevation, but the complete door frame/opening is not clearly visible.

Do not return OMITTED when partial door-like geometry is visible. If a possible full-height rectangular door/opening or diagonal door-leaf graphic is partially visible but not enough to confidently validate the door scope, return REVIEW_REQUIRED.
omit_condition: The detected view is not an Elevation.

Return OMITTED only when the Elevation contains no visible door-like evidence at all: no full-height rectangular opening, no tall side rectangular opening, no diagonal door-leaf graphic, no door frame, and no wall-opening shape.
The note appears elsewhere on the sheet but not in this exact Elevation view.

reasoning.description: Explain why the rule returned PASS, FAIL, OMITTED, or REVIEW_REQUIRED based on the presence of door scope and door by others note.

view_results.view_type: Detected actual view title from drawing
